description: Schema for NEN Release Item details. The 'releaseItem' corresponds to the 'Manifestation' level of the FRBR model: 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Functional_Requirements_for_Bibliographic_Records' One 'publication' has several 'releaseItems', each being different renditions of the same intellectual content in various formats or languages. One 'releaseItem' refers to zero or one downloadable content via the 'contentRef' sub-element.
title: NenReleaseItem Canonical Schema
$comment: Last modified on 2026-03-06 by Hans Bot - NEN. Derived from 'NEN Content Publication V0.8.1-bundled.yaml'

releaseItemType.description: Qualification of the release item. Types may be concatenated with '+' delimiters

fileFormat.description: The capitalized content format type of the NEN release item.

fileFormatVariant.description: Additional specification to declare the correct XML Variant

numPages.description: Approximate number of pages of the content at the URL, if applicable (e.g., for PDF documents).

version.description: Version number of the NEN release item, starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each new version.

releaseDate.description: Release date of the NEN release item in the European format (YYYY-MM-DD).

expirationDate.description: Expiration date of the NEN release item in the European format (YYYY-MM-DD).

language.description: Language of the content at the URL, represented as an ISO 639-1 2 letter code.

isMultiLingual.description: The item is or isn't part of a multilingual publication
isMultiLingual.$comment: From the description it seems more senisible to manage this attribute at the publication level

contentRef.description: URL where the content of the NEN release item can be accessed. Normally all releaseItems do have content, but there are exceptions when: the content is not available online (a physical-only, paper publication), or the content is online but not downloadable (an online database, eg. ISO 7010). Therefore this element is optional.

fileName.description: string the name without the “dot”and extension of the file to be saved
fileName.$comment: No pattern added; unsure of naming convention used. Internal reference: https://nendelft.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/Normenbank/pages/144670901/nenContentRefFileName+-+moved+from+ContentRef+container.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the content at the URL.

contentLength.description: Number of bytes of the content at the URL. May be used to make sure the complete stream was downloaded.

checksum.description: A signature of the content at the URL, that may be used to (1) detect changes more reliably and simply than using the timestamp - because it really matches the actual content, (2) ensure that the metadata description corresponds effectively to the content it refers via the url, and (3) ensure the data was neither corrupted during upload nor download, or altered during storage

publication.$comment: can be used as path parameter in link definition in the API
